acceptance_targets: [ch07_f01_example_line_echo] Pass if the revised exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:247 no longer uses the recognizable structure 'Nobody tipped [there/at the Golden Ox]. She did.' or any close paraphrase of the style-bible sample, while still making clear by the end of the beat that Faye reads the tip as evidence the other woman knew service work. Fail if a cold reader can still map the revised wording directly back to the calibration line.

[ch07_f02_records_office_handout] Pass if, after reading the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:331, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:351, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:397, a cold reader can answer three questions from the chapter alone: why this office can access the audit, why Faye is eligible to receive it now, and why the misuse did not already arrive with her revival paperwork. Fail if any of those answers still requires off-page inference.

[ch07_f03_dialogue_register_flattening] Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:243 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:413 gives Miriam at least two lines whose vocabulary could only belong to a career waitress and the clerk at least two lines whose vocabulary could only belong to a bureaucrat, and if at least one major exchange in either scene ends in interruption or unresolved friction instead of a neat explanatory closer. Fail if the highlighted beats could still be swapped between Miriam and the clerk with little loss.

[ch07_f04_afterhours_redundancy] Pass if every paragraph in the revised closing sequence starting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:565 introduces at least one of the following: a new inference, a new material consequence, or a new decision/next-step pressure that was not already clear by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:557. Fail if consecutive paragraphs still merely restate that another woman lived under Faye's name.

[ch07_f05_negation_correction_tic] Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:263, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:605 contain no more than two key realizations delivered through the explicit negation/correction template, and at least one of the three beats is rendered through concrete image or action instead. Fail if all three moments still resolve in the same sentence mold.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:247 no longer uses the recognizable structure 'Nobody tipped [there/at the Golden Ox]. She did.' or any close paraphrase of the style-bible sample, while still making clear by the end of the beat that Faye reads the tip as evidence the other woman knew service work. Fail if a cold reader can still map the revised wording directly back to the calibration line.
findings.problem: The tip exchange paraphrases a calibration-only Faye example from outline/style_bible.json rather than generating fresh scene-born speech. The beat at lines 247-251 reproduces the distinctive skeleton of the forbidden sample about nobody tipping at the Golden Ox, which the review contract explicitly treats as a HIGH issue.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recompose the exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:247 so Faye's fixation on the tip comes out of this exact diner pressure, not the style-bible wording. Preserve the dramatic function, but replace the recognizable 'nobody tipped there / she did' structure at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:247 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:251 with a new attack line, inference, or misread that is not an echo of the calibration sample.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after reading the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:331, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:351, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:397, a cold reader can answer three questions from the chapter alone: why this office can access the audit, why Faye is eligible to receive it now, and why the misuse did not already arrive with her revival paperwork. Fail if any of those answers still requires off-page inference.
findings.problem: The records-office turn is locally vivid but under-justified. After a standard form and current biometrics, a public Mars office can pull an Earth-side cryogenic misconduct audit plus an eleven-year utilization log, but the chapter never makes clear why this office has that jurisdiction, why Faye qualifies for the pull now, or why the same misuse was not surfaced at revival. Because the chapter's central discovery depends on this access path, the reveal reads partly like a plot handout.
findings.rewrite_direction: At the setup around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:331 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:351, or in the reveal around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:397, add the missing causal chain: what legal or administrative channel routes suspended-patient fraud reviews through Mars civil identity, why current biometric and voice confirmation are enough for Faye to unlock it now, and why the file was not proactively delivered when she woke. Keep the shabby-office texture, but make the access logic explicit on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised material around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:243 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:413 gives Miriam at least two lines whose vocabulary could only belong to a career waitress and the clerk at least two lines whose vocabulary could only belong to a bureaucrat, and if at least one major exchange in either scene ends in interruption or unresolved friction instead of a neat explanatory closer. Fail if the highlighted beats could still be swapped between Miriam and the clerk with little loss.
findings.problem: Miriam and the records clerk converge into the same polished dry-deadpan register, and both major conversations repeatedly resolve through the same question-to-translation-to-neat-closer ladder. Miriam's 'For what it's worth' beat and the clerk's 'If you want outrage,' 'Why costs extra,' and 'just making me late' all read like authored mini-speeches in a shared cadence rather than two distinct side-character voices under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the diner exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:243 and the office exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:413. Let Miriam stay rooted in service-work habit, customer-reading, and counter rhythm, while the clerk stays procedural, scope-limiting, and institution-shaped. Break at least one of the clean explanation ladders at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:295, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:431, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:519 with interruption, refusal, misread, or physical action so the scenes stop resolving every prompt into a quotable line.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if every paragraph in the revised closing sequence starting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:565 introduces at least one of the following: a new inference, a new material consequence, or a new decision/next-step pressure that was not already clear by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:557. Fail if consecutive paragraphs still merely restate that another woman lived under Faye's name.
findings.problem: The after-hours records-office coda keeps restating the same identity fracture after the chapter has already landed it. Once the clerk leaves and Faye has the papers, the passage from lines 565-645 cycles through office language versus lived life, the other woman's survival, the lack of a scar, and the split ownership of the name without adding enough new fact, decision, or external consequence to justify its length.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or refocus the closing sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:565. Keep the sit-with-it beat required by the chapter spine, but make each paragraph after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:565 earn its space by adding either a new inference not already explicit by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:533, a fresh physical consequence, or a concrete next-step pressure. Cut restatements that only rephrase the same wound.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:263, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:605 contain no more than two key realizations delivered through the explicit negation/correction template, and at least one of the three beats is rendered through concrete image or action instead. Fail if all three moments still resolve in the same sentence mold.
findings.problem: The chapter lands key observations three times in the same negation-then-correction mold: 'Not older. Just used harder,' 'Not weak. Just tired,' and 'Not memory ... Just accumulated use.' Once or twice would read as choice; by the third recurrence it registers as a prose tic and starts flattening the chapter's rhythm.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:263, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:605 so they do not all arrive through the same 'Not X. Just Y.' correction. Keep the insight, but let at least one of these moments land through image, action, or a different sentence shape.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise demands tonal range that most novels avoid: genuine slapstick comedy in the same book as genuine grief, syndicate violence alongside domestic warmth, existential identity crisis alongside arguments about noodles. The risk is not in the dark material — it's in the tonal juxtaposition. Commit to both registers fully. A comic scene should be actually funny, not wryly amusing. A grief scene should be actually painful, not tastefully sad. The Bebop register earns its power by refusing to choose between comedy and tragedy — they coexist in the same room, the same conversation, the same character. Do not smooth the transitions between registers. Let them be jarring when the material is jarring. Let a joke land in the middle of a painful conversation if that's what the character would do. Let a painful truth arrive during a comic scene without the comedy stopping to make room for it. The creative risk is trust: trust that the reader can hold both, trust that tonal instability is a form of emotional honesty, not a failure of control.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. When a scene arrives at a moment of physical, emotional, or moral consequence, the prose must stay present — naming, rendering, inhabiting. Summary, vagueness, and decorative circumlocution at the point of impact are disallowed. If a character is hurt, drunk, broke, grieving, or afraid, render the specific texture of that experience. 'She was devastated' is not writing. 'She found his grocery list on the fridge' is writing. The Bebop world is broke, dangerous, and populated by people who have lost things they cannot get back. The prose must honor that.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific precision. The Bebop crew lives in contractions; full forms should feel conspicuous when they appear.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Consecutive chapters must differ in at least two of: scene count, dialogue density, pacing, primary emotional register, and structural shape. A dialogue-heavy chapter should be followed by one with more interiority or action. A multi-scene chapter should be followed by one with fewer scenes or a different temporal rhythm. Vary paragraph length distribution across chapters — some chapters should run long and flowing, others should be built from shorter, harder units. The novel's rhythm at the chapter level should feel like a jazz set: different tempos, different keys, with throughlines that connect them.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, vernacular-adjacent, unsentimental. The narration should sound like an extension of the characters' world — working-class, spacefaring, slightly retro-future. Avoid elevated literary diction unless the passage specifically calls for it (rare). Avoid clinical or academic language. The narrator is not above the characters; the narrator is in the room with them. Latinate vocabulary should be rare and purposeful. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred for physical description.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating by chapter or scene as indicated in the outline. The camera sits behind the POV character's eyes — we see what they see, hear their internal register in the narration, and do not access other characters' thoughts except through observed behavior. When the chapter is ensemble, the camera moves between characters within scenes but does not overlay multiple interiorities simultaneously. Maintain enough distance that the narrator's voice is present alongside the character's — this is not stream of consciousness but inhabited third person.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Jazz-inflected variation. Sentences should vary aggressively in length and structure — short declaratives next to longer compound sentences, fragments where they create rhythmic force (but never elliptical clipping). The Bebop register favors syncopation: beat expectations set up and then broken. Avoid metronomic alternation (short-long-short-long). Let passages breathe with their own rhythms. Action sequences should accelerate sentence pace. Reflective passages should slow and lengthen. Transitions between registers should be controlled, not abrupt.
